title: Business Analyst Pharma (m/w/d)
required_skills: Experience in pharmaceutical company, healthcare/pharma industry, or pharmaceutical market research
Degree in economics, data science, statistics, or natural sciences
Strong communication and service orientation for pharma clients
High data affinity and strong MS Office skills (Excel, PowerPoint)
Very good German and English (min. C1)
matched_skills: Power BI PL-300 certified — directly matches bonus Power BI requirement
Excel and data analysis: ECE Group spreadsheet optimisation, HSBC dashboards
Competitive analysis and market sizing: ECE Group market intelligence for automotive/furniture retail
Executive-ready reports: ECE Group, HSBC data storytelling
missing_skills: Pharmaceutical market data knowledge (Rx, drug data)
German C1/C2 (Boru is B1 — role requires very good German)
core_responsibilities: Handle customer requests at interface of customer, sales, and internal services
Develop customer-oriented data solutions and tools in Market Data & Intelligence team
Identify trends and market potentials in pharmaceutical market research
Structured analyses, clear visualisations, and targeted presentations
recommended_emphasis: Power BI certification as technical differentiator for data visualisation work
ECE Group competitive analysis and market sizing as analog market research experience
HSBC data storytelling for executive audiences — structured insight delivery
Cross-functional project involvement across ECE and Hapag-Lloyd roles
summary: Score 32.5 (weak match). Boru's Power BI certification and market analysis experience (ECE Group) are genuine strengths for this data-focused analyst role. However, there are critical gaps: no pharma/healthcare domain knowledge, German is only B1 while the role requires C1, and no R/Python skills. The domain barrier (pharma market data expertise) is substantial. Proceed with caution and only if the candidate is open to a steep domain learning curve.
